what: Plot all traits with a loop
title: gg_manhattan()
text: simple loop can used plot GWAS results trait within specified folder.
code: # Create for loop for(i in list_Traits(folder = "GWAS_Results/")) {   # Plot   mp <- gg_Manhattan(     # Specify a folder with GWAS results     folder = "GWAS_Results/",      # Select a trait to plot     trait = i )   # Save   ggsave(paste0("figures/gg_Manhattan_01_", i, ".png"),           mp, width = 12, height = 3.5, bg = "white") }

title: gg_manhattan()
text: default setting gg_Manhattan() set facet = F plot models together. changing facet = T can separate GWAS model. setting facet = T, points now colored chromosome, can changed chr.colors. Significant associations can also highlighted setting highlight.sig sig.color.
code: # Plot mp <- gg_Manhattan(   # Specify a folder with GWAS results   folder = "GWAS_Results/",   # Select a trait to plot   trait = "Cotyledon_RedvsYellow",    # Facet out the different GWAS models   facet = T,   # Set chromosome colors   chr.colors = rep(c("darkgreen", "darkgoldenrod3"), 4),   # Highlight significant associations   highlight.sig = T,   # Choose the highlight color   sig.color = "darkred" ) # Save ggsave("figures/gg_Manhattan_02_Cotyledon_RedvsYellow.png",         mp, width = 12, height = 8, bg = "white")

title: gg_manhattan()
text: single chromosome can plotted setting chr. x-axis can also adjusted setting chr.unit.
code: # Plot mp <- gg_Manhattan(   # Specify a folder with GWAS results   folder = "GWAS_Results/",    # Select a trait to plot   trait = "DTF_Nepal_2017",   # Select a chromosome to plot   chr = 2,   # Change chromosome units   chr.unit = "50 Mbp" ) # Save ggsave("figures/gg_Manhattan_03_DTF_Nepal_2017.png",         mp, width = 7, height = 3.5, bg = "white")

title: gg_manhattan()
text: default gg_Manhattan() set plot title = trait, can changed. can also adjust threshold add asug.threshold. specified, markers can highlighted labels customized. Adding vlines can used aid comparisons manhattan plots accross GWAS models. vlines can customized vline.colors vline.types. can Select GWAS models plot set colors model.colors. Significant associations can also highlighted setting highlight.sig sig.color. QQ plot can also removed setting addQQ = F.
code: # Plot mp <- gg_Manhattan(   # Specify a folder with GWAS results   folder = "GWAS_Results/",   # Select a trait to plot   trait = "DTF_Nepal_2017",   # Create a title for the plot   title = "DTF Sask 2017",   # Set horizontal thresholds bars   threshold = 6.7,   sug.threshold = 5,   # Highlight specific markers   markers = c("Lcu.1GRN.Chr2p44545877",              "Lcu.1GRN.Chr5p1658484"),   labels = c("p44545877",              "p1658484"),   # Add vertical lines   vlines = c("Lcu.1GRN.Chr2p44545877",              "Lcu.1GRN.Chr5p1658484",              "Lcu.1GRN.Chr6p3269280"),   vline.colors = c("red","red","blue"),   vline.types = c(1,1,2),   # Change the legend alignment   legend.box="vertical",   # Select GWAS models   models = c("MLM", "MLMM", "FarmCPU", "BLINK"),   # Set colors for each GWAS model   model.colors = c("darkgreen", "darkred", "darkorange3", "steelblue"),   # Highlight significant associations   highlight.sig = T,   # Highlight color   sig.color = "black",   # Remove the QQ plot   addQQ = F) # Save ggsave("figures/gg_Manhattan_04_DTF_Nepal_2017.png",         mp, width = 8, height = 3.5, bg = "white") # Plot mp <- gg_Manhattan(   # Specify a folder with GWAS results   folder = "GWAS_Results/",   # Select a trait to plot   trait = "DTF_Sask_2017_b",   # Create a title for the plot   title = "DTF Sask 2017 | Covariate = b",   # Set horizontal thresholds bars   threshold = 6.7,   sug.threshold = 5,   # Highlight specific markers   markers = "Lcu.1GRN.Chr6p3269280",   labels = "p3269280",   # Add vertical lines   vlines = c("Lcu.1GRN.Chr2p44545877",              "Lcu.1GRN.Chr5p1658484",              "Lcu.1GRN.Chr6p3269280"),   vline.colors = c("red","red","blue"),   vline.types = c(2,2,1),   # Change the legend alignment   legend.box="vertical",   # Select GWAS models   models = c("MLM", "MLMM", "FarmCPU", "BLINK"),   # Set colors for each GWAS model   model.colors = c("darkgreen", "darkred", "darkorange3", "steelblue"),   # Highlight significant associations   highlight.sig = T,   # Highlight color   sig.color = "black",   # Remove the QQ plot   addQQ = F) # Save ggsave("figures/gg_Manhattan_04_DTF_Sask_2017_b.png",         mp, width = 8, height = 3.5, bg = "white")

title: Authors and Citation
text: Wright D (2026). gwaspr: GWAS plotting R. R package version 0.9.0, https://derekmichaelwright.github.io/gwaspr/.
code: @Manual{,   title = {gwaspr: GWAS plotting in R},   author = {Derek Wright},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.9.0},   url = {https://derekmichaelwright.github.io/gwaspr/}, }

title: GWAS plotting in R
text: gwaspr: R package plotting GWAS results GAPIT package

title: GWAS plotting in R
code: devtools::install_github("derekmichaelwright/gwaspr") library(gwaspr)

title: GWAS plotting in R
text: GAPIT: R package performing Genome Wide Association Studies (GWAS) GAPIT Website: https://www.maizegenetics.net/gapit GAPIT github: https://github.com/jiabowang/GAPIT

title: GWAS plotting in R
text: tidyverse, ggpubr, ggbeeswarm, ggrepel, ggtext, plotly, htmlwidgets

title: clean_GWAS_Results — clean_GWAS_Results
code: clean_GWAS_Results(   folder = "GWAS_Results/",   remove_nonResults = T,   remove_Kansas = F,   remove_NYC = F )

title: clean_GWAS_Results — clean_GWAS_Results
text: folder Folder containing GWAS results. remove_nonResults Delelets non .csv files (.pdf files) remove_Kansas Delete Kansas files. remove_NYC Delete NYC files.

title: convert_IUPAC — convert_IUPAC
text: Converts hapmap formatted genotype files IUPAC codes.

title: convert_IUPAC — convert_IUPAC
code: convert_IUPAC(xg, toIUPAC = T)

title: convert_IUPAC — convert_IUPAC
text: toIUPAC Logical, convert IUPAC (TRUE) IUPAC (FALSE). xG GWAS genotype object. Note: needs hapmap format.

title: dropNAcol — dropNAcol
code: dropNAcol(x)

title: gg_GWAS_Hits — gg_GWAS_Hits
text: Creates summary GWAS plot significant associations.

title: gg_GWAS_Hits — gg_GWAS_Hits
code: gg_GWAS_Hits(   xx,   xG,   xCV = NULL,   traits,   range = 2e+06,   title = "",   sigMin = 0,   models = c("BLINK", "FarmCPU", "MLMM", "MLM", "GLM", "CMLM", "SUPER"),   model.colors = gwaspr_Colors,   model.shapes = c(21:25, 21:22),   vlines = NULL,   vline.colors = rep("red", length(vlines)),   vline.types = rep(1, length(vlines)),   legend.rows = 1,   facet = F,   caption = T )

title: gg_GWAS_Hits — gg_GWAS_Hits
text: xx Table significant GWAS results. See ?table_GWAS_Results(). xG Genotype data hapmap format. xCV Optional, filtering "CV" column. traits List traits use. range Range binning GWAS hits. title Title horizontal facet. sigMin Minimum number hits plot. models Models read. model.colors Colors model. vlines Markers labelled vertical red line. vline.colors colors vertical line. vline.types lty vertical line. legend.rows Number rows legend. facet Logical. facet GWAS model. caption Logical. caption added.

title: gg_GWAS_Summary — gg_GWAS_Summary
text: Creates summary GWAS plot significant associations. Note: function requires GWAS results files ordered

title: gg_GWAS_Summary — gg_GWAS_Summary
code: gg_GWAS_Summary(   folder = "GWAS_Results/",   traits = list_Traits(folder),   groups = NULL,   threshold = round(-log10(5e-08), 1),   sug.threshold = round(-log10(5e-06), 1),   chr = NULL,   pos1 = NULL,   pos2 = NULL,   models = c("MLM", "MLMM", "FarmCPU", "BLINK", "CMLM", "SUPER"),   model.colors = gwaspr_Colors,   shapes = 21:25,   hlines = NULL,   vlines = NULL,   vline.colors = rep("red", length(vlines)),   vline.types = rep(1, length(vlines)),   vline.legend = T,   threshold.legend = T,   title = "Summary of Significant GWAS Results",   caption = NULL,   rowread = 2000,   legend.position = "bottom",   legend.rows = 1,   plotHBPvalues = F,   skyline = "Kansas" )

title: gg_GWAS_Summary — gg_GWAS_Summary
text: folder Folder containing GWAS results. traits traits read. groups Grouping traits. equal length traits. threshold Significant threshold. sug.threshold Suggestive threshold. chr Chromosomes plot. pos1 starting position plot. pos2 ending position plot. models Models read. model.colors Colors model. shapes shape values use different models. e.g., 21:25. hlines Locations horizontal lines. e.g., hlines = c(1.5,2.5). vlines Markers labelled vertical red line. vline.colors colors vertical line. vline.types lty vertical line. vline.legend Logical, display vline color legend. threshold.legend Logical, display vline color legend. title title plot. caption caption plot. rowread Number rows read GWAS results file. legend.rows Number rows legend. plotHBPvalues Logical, H.B.P.Values uses. skyline skyline type use. Can "NYC" "Kansas". left NULL, use highest P.value.

title: gg_LDHeatmap — gg_LDheatmap
code: gg_LDheatmap(   xg = myG,   chr = 6,   pos1 = 0,   pos2 = 6e+06,   myMs = NULL,   myTitle = NULL,   axisTextSize = NULL,   nameTrim = NULL )

title: gg_LDHeatmap — gg_LDheatmap
text: chr Chromosome plot. pos1 Start position within selected chromosome. pos2 End position within selected chromosome. myMs Markers highlight within plot. myTitle Title plot. axisTextSize Text size axis labels (genotype names). nameTrim String used trim marker names. xG GWAS genotype object. Note: needs hapmap format.

title: gg_Manhattan — gg_Manhattan
text: Create manhattan plots GAPIT GWAS results.

title: gg_Manhattan — gg_Manhattan
code: gg_Manhattan(   folder = "GWAS_Results/",   trait = list_Traits(folder)[1],   title = trait,   threshold = NULL,   sug.threshold = NULL,   chr = NULL,   markers = NULL,   labels = markers,   vlines = markers,   vline.colors = rep("red", length(vlines)),   vline.types = rep(1, length(vlines)),   legend = T,   legend.rows = 1,   legend.box = "horizontal",   point.sizes = c(0.3, 1, 0.75),   pmax = NULL,   pmin = 0,   models = c("MLM", "MLMM", "FarmCPU", "BLINK", "GLM", "CMLM", "SUPER"),   model.colors = c("darkgreen", "darkred", "darkorange3", "steelblue", "darkorchid4",     "blue2", "magenta3"),   facet = F,   highlight.sig = F,   sig.color = "black",   chr.colors = rep(c("darkgreen", "darkgoldenrod3"), 30),   chr.unit = "100 Mbp",   plotHBPvalues = F,   skyline = "Kansas",   addQQ = T )

title: gg_Manhattan — gg_Manhattan
text: folder Folder containing GWAS results. trait trait read. title title plot. threshold Significant Threshold. sug.threshold Suggested threshold. chr Chromosomes plot. Use want plot single chromosome. markers Markers labelled. labels Labels used markers. vlines Markers used location vertical lines. vline.colors colors vertical line. vline.types lty vertical line. legend Logical, whether add legend. legend.rows Number rows legend. legend.box Alignment legend. Default "horizontal", can changed "vertical". point.sizes Sizes points. c("Sig", "Sig", "Sug"). pmax max value y-axis. Markers higher values lowered pmax.. pmin min Value plotting. Markers lower values removed. models Models read. model.colors Colors model. Used facet = F. facet Logical, whether produce facetted multi-model plot. Default facet = F. highlight.sig Logical, whether highlight significant associations black circle. Used facet = F. sig.color Color significant assoctiations. Used facet = T. chr.colors Colors chromosome. Used facet = T. chr.unit Unit x-axis. Can one c("kbp","100 kbp","Mbp","100 Mbp","Gbp"). plotHBPvalues Logical, TRUE, H.B.P.values uses. skyline skyline type use. Can "NYC" "Kansas". NULL, use highest P.value. addQQ Logical, whether add QQ plot.

title: gg_Manhattan_Zoom — gg_Manhattan_Zoom
text: Create manhattan plots GAPIT GWAS results zoomed specific region chromosome.

title: gg_Manhattan_Zoom — gg_Manhattan_Zoom
code: gg_Manhattan_Zoom(   folder = "GWAS_Results/",   trait = list_Traits(folder)[1],   title = trait,   chr = 1,   pos1 = NULL,   pos2 = NULL,   addGenome = T,   threshold = NULL,   sug.threshold = NULL,   markers = NULL,   labels = markers,   vlines = markers,   vline.colors = rep("red", length(vlines)),   vline.types = rep(1, length(vlines)),   vline.legend = T,   facet = F,   pmax = NULL,   models = c("MLM", "MLMM", "FarmCPU", "BLINK", "GLM", "CMLM", "SUPER"),   model.colors = c("darkgreen", "darkred", "darkorange3", "steelblue", "darkorchid4",     "blue2", "magenta3"),   sig.color = "black",   legend.rows = 1,   legend.box = "horizontal",   point.sizes = c(0.3, 1, 0.75),   plotHBPvalues = F,   skyline = "Kansas" )

title: gg_Manhattan_Zoom — gg_Manhattan_Zoom
text: folder Folder containing GWAS results. trait trait read. title title plot. chr Chromosome plot. pos1 Start position chromosome. pos2 End position chromosome. threshold Significant Threshold. sug.threshold Suggested threshold. markers Markers labelled. labels Labels used markers. vlines Markers used location vertical lines. vline.colors colors vertical line. vline.types lty vertical line. vline.legend Logical, whether add legend vlines. facet Logical, whether produce facetted multi-model plot. Default facet = F. pmax max value y-axis. models Models read. model.colors Colors model. Used facet = F. sig.color Color significant assoctiations. legend.rows Number rows legend. legend.box Alignment legend. Default "horizontal", can changed "vertical". point.sizes Sizes points. c("Sig", "Sig", "Sug"). plotHBPvalues Logical, H.B.P.Values uses. skyline skyline type use. Can "NYC" "Kansas". left NULL, use highest P.value.

text: folder Folder containing GWAS results. trait trait read. title title plot. threshold Significant Threshold. sug.threshold Suggested threshold. chr Chromosomes plot. Use want plot single chromosome. markers Markers labelled. labels Labels used markers. vlines Markers used location vertical lines. vline.colors colors vertical line. vline.types lty vertical line. vline.legend Logical, whether add legend vlines. addQQ Logical, whether add QQ plot pmax max value y-axis. models Models read. sig.col Color significant assoctiations. chr.colors Colors chromosome. chr.unit Unit x-axis. title: gg_QTL_Summary — gg_QTL_Summary
text: Creates summary QTL plot significant associations.

title: gg_QTL_Summary — gg_QTL_Summary
code: gg_QTL_Summary(   myG,   myQ,   title = "Summary of QTL Results",   lodFill = F,   fillColor = "darkgreen",   fillColor_low = "grey50",   xLab = "Pos",   facetLab = "lg" )

title: gg_QTL_Summary — gg_QTL_Summary
text: myG Genetic map. Must contain following 3 columns c("Marker","Chr","Pos"). myQ Table QTL results. Must contain following 5 columns c("Trait","Chr","Pos","Pos_lo","Pos_hi","lod"). title Custom title plot. lodFill Logical. true, fill color gradient based lod values. fillColor Color filling points. fillColor_low lodfill = T, low end gradeint color scale filling points. xLab Custom x lab. facetLab Custom label facets.

title: gg_QTL_Summary_Groups — gg_QTL_Summary_Groups
text: Creates summary QTL plot significant associations.

title: gg_QTL_Summary_Groups — gg_QTL_Summary_Groups
code: gg_QTL_Summary_Groups(   myQ,   myG,   title = "Summary of QTL Results",   yGroup,   yLab = NULL,   facetGroup,   colorGroup,   colorName = NULL,   fillColors = gwaspr_Colors,   xLab = "Pos",   facetLab = "lg" )

title: gg_QTL_Summary_Groups — gg_QTL_Summary_Groups
text: myQ Table QTL results. Must contain following 5 columns c("Trait","Chr","Pos","Pos_lo","Pos_hi","lod"). must also include grouping columns set user yGroup,facetGroup & colorGroup. myG Genetic map. Must contain following 3 columns c("Marker","Chr","Pos"). title Custom title plot. yGroup Name column y axis grouping. yLab Custom y-axis label. facetGroup Name column facet grouping. colorGroup Name column color grouping. colorName Title color legend. fillColors Color filling points. xLab Custom x-axis label. facetLab Custom label facets.

title: gg_Volcano — gg_Volcano
